verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Afterward Jesus findeth him in the Temple, and said unto him, Behold, thou art made whole; sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: A fearful thing is sin, fearful, and the ruin of the soul, and the mischief oftentimes through its excess has overflowed and attacked men’s bodies also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since for the most part when the soul is diseased we feel no pain, but if the body receive though but a little hurt, we use every exertion to free it from its infirmity, because we are sensible of the infirmity, therefore God oftentimes punisheth the body for the transgressions of the soul, so that by means of the scourging of the inferior part, the better part also may receive some healing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus too among the Corinthians Paul restored the adulterer, checking the disease of the soul by the destruction of the flesh, and having applied the knife to the body, so repressed the evil ( 1 Cor. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 5 ); like some excellent physician employing external cautery for dropsy or spleen, when they refuse to yield to internal remedies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This also Christ did in the case of the paralytic; as He showed when He said, “Behold, thou art made whole; sin no more, lest a worse thing come unto thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now what do we learn from this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: First, that his disease had been produced by his sins; secondly, that the accounts of hell fire are to be believed; thirdly, that the punishment is long, nay endless.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Where now are those who say, “I murdered in an hour, I committed adultery in a little moment of time, and am I eternally punished?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For behold this man had not sinned for so many years as he suffered, for he had spent a whole lifetime in the length of his punishment; and sins are not judged by time, but by the nature of the transgressions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides this, we may see another thing, that though we have suffered severely for former sins, if we afterwards fall into the same, we shall suffer much more severely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And with good reason; for he who is not made better even by punishment, is afterwards led as insensible and a despiser to still heavier chastisement.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The fault should of itself be sufficient to check and to render more sober the man who once has slipped, but when not even the addition of punishment effects this, he naturally requires more bitter torments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now if even in this world when after punishment we fall into the same sins, we are chastised yet more severely then before, ought we not when after sinning we have not been punished at all, to be then very exceedingly afraid and to tremble, as being about to endure something irreparable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And wherefore,” saith some one, “are not all thus punished? for we see many bad men well in body, vigorous, and enjoying great prosperity.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But let us not be confident, let us mourn for them in this case most of all, since their having suffered nothing here, helps them on to a severer vengeance hereafter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As Paul declares when he saith, “But now that we are judged, we are chastened of the Lord, that we should not be condemned with the world” ( 1 Cor. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 32 ); for the punishments here are for warning, there for vengeance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What then,” saith one, “do all diseases proceed from sin?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not all, but most of them; and some proceed from different kinds of loose living, since gluttony, intemperance, and sloth, produce such like sufferings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the one rule we have to observe, is to bear every stroke thankfully; for they are sent because of our sins, as in the Kings we see one attacked by gout ( 1 Kings xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 23 ); they are sent also to make us approved, as the Lord saith to Job, “Thinkest thou that I have spoken to thee, save that thou mightest appear righteous?” ( Job lx.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why is it that in the case of these paralytics Christ bringeth forward their sins?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He saith also to him in Matthew who lay on a bed, “Son, be of good cheer, thy sins are forgiven thee” ( Matt. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 2 ): and to this man, “Behold, thou art made whole; sin no more.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I know that some slander this paralytic, asserting that he was an accuser of Christ, and that therefore this speech was addressed to him; what then shall we say of the other in Matthew, who heard nearly the same words?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For Christ saith to him also, “Thy sins be forgiven thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence it is clear, that neither was this man thus addressed on the account which they allege.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this we may see more clearly from what follows; for, saith the Evangelist, “Afterward Jesus findeth him in the Temple,” which is an indication of his great piety; for he departed not into the market places and walks, nor gave himself up to luxury and ease, but remained in the Temple, although about to sustain so violent an attack and to be harassed by all there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet none of these things persuaded him to depart from the Temple.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Moreover Christ having found him, even after he had conversed with the Jews, implied nothing of the kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had He desired to charge him with this, He would have said to him, “Art thou again attempting the same sins as before, art thou not made better by thy cure?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet He said nothing of the kind, but merely secureth him for the future.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then, when He had cured the halt and maimed, did He not in any instance make mention of the like?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Methinks that the diseases of these (the paralytic) arose from acts of sin, those of the others from natural infirmity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or if this be not so, then by means of these men, and by the words spoken to them, He hath spoken to the rest also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since this disease is more grievous than any other, by the greater He correcteth also the less.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And as when He had healed a certain other He charged him to give glory to God, addressing this exhortation not to him only but through him to all, so He addresseth to these, and by these to all the rest of mankind, that exhortation and advice which was given to them by word of mouth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides this we may also say, that Jesus perceived great endurance in his soul, and addressed the exhortation to him as to one who was able to receive His command, keeping him to health both by the benefit, and by the fear of future ills.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe the absence of boasting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “Behold, I have made thee whole,” but, “Thou art made whole; sin no more.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, not, “lest I punish thee,” but, “lest a worse thing come unto thee”; putting both expressions not personally, and showing that the cure was rather of grace than of merit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He declared not to him that he was delivered after suffering the deserved amount of punishment, but that through lovingkindness he was made whole.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Had this not been the case, He would have said, “Behold, thou hast suffered a sufficient punishment for thy sins, be thou steadfast for the future.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But now He spake not so, but how?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Behold, thou art made whole; sin no more.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us continually repeat these words to ourselves, and if after having been chastised we have been delivered, let each say to himself, “Behold, thou art made whole; sin no more.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if we suffer not punishment though continuing in the same courses, let us use for our charm that word of the Apostle, “The goodness of God leadeth [us] to repentance, but after [our] hardness and impenitent heart, [we] treasure up unto [ourselves] wrath.” ( Rom. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And not only by strengthening the sick man’s body, but also in another way, did He afford him a strong proof of His Divinity; for by saying, “Sin no more,” He showed that He knew all the transgressions that had formerly been committed by him; and by this He would gain his belief as to the future.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The man departed, and told the Jews that it was Jesus that had made him whole.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again observe him continuing in the same right feeling.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith not, “This is he who said, Take up thy bed,” but when they continually advanced this seeming charge, he continually puts forward the defense, again declaring his Healer, and seeking to attract and attach others to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he was not so unfeeling as after such a benefit and charge to betray his Benefactor, and to speak as he did with an evil intention.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Had he been a wild beast, had he been something unlike a man and of stone, the benefit and the fear would have been enough to restrain him, since, having the threat lodged within, he would have dreaded lest he should suffer “a worse thing,” having already received the greatest pledges of the power of his Physician.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, had he wished to slander Him, he would have said nothing about his own cure, but would have mentioned and urged against Him the breach of the Sabbath.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this is not the case, surely it is not; the words are words of great boldness and candor; he pro claims his Benefactor no less than the blind man did.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what said he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He made clay, and anointed mine eyes” ( c. ix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 6 ); and so this man of whom we now speak, “It is Jesus who made me whole.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Therefore did the Jews persecute Jesus, and sought to slay Him, because He had done these things on the Sabbath day.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “My Father worketh hitherto, and I work.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When there was need to make excuse for the Disciples, He brought forward David their fellow-servant, saying, “Have ye not read what David did when he was an hungered?” ( Matt. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when excuse was to be made for Himself, He betook Himself to the Father, showing in two ways His Equality, by calling God His Father peculiarly, and by doing the same things which He did.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And wherefore did He not mention what took place at Jericho ?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because He wished to raise them up from earth that they might no longer attend to Him as to a man, but as to God, and as to one who ought to legislate: since had He not been The Very Son and of the same Essence, the defense would have been worse than the charge.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if a viceroy who had altered a royal law should, when charged with so doing, excuse himself in this manner, and say, “Yea, for the king also has annulled laws,” he would not be able to escape, but would thus increase the weight of the charge.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But in this instance, since the dignity is equal, the defense is made perfect on most secure grounds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “From the charges,” saith He, “from which ye absolve God, absolve Me also.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore He said first, “My Father,” that He might persuade them even against their will to allow to Him the same, through reverence of His clearly asserted Sonship.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If any one say, “And how doth the Father ‘work,’ who ceased on the seventh day from all His works?” let him learn the manner in which He “worketh.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then is the manner of His working?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He careth for, He holdeth together all that hath been made.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore when thou beholdest the sun rising and the moon running in her path, the lakes, and fountains, and rivers, and rains, the course of nature in the seeds and in our own bodies and those of irrational beings, and all the rest by means of which this universe is made up, then learn the ceaseless working of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For He maketh His sun to rise upon the evil and the good, and sendeth rain on the just and on the unjust.” ( Matt. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again; “If God so clothe the grass of the field, which to-day is, and to-morrow is cast into the fire” ( Matt. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 30 ); and speaking of the birds He said, “Your Heavenly Father feedeth them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In that place then He did all on the Sabbath day by words only, and added nothing more, but refuted their charges by what was done in the Temple and from their own practice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But here where He commanded a work to be done, the taking up a bed, (a thing of no great importance as regarded the miracle, though by it He showed one point, a manifest violation of the Sabbath,) He leads up His discourse to something greater, desiring the more to awe them by reference to the dignity of the Father, and to lead them up to higher thought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore when His discourse is concerning the Sabbath, He maketh not His defense as man only, or as God only, but sometimes in one way, sometimes in the other; because He desired to persuade them both of the condescension of the Dispensation, and the Dignity of His Godhead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He now defendeth Himself as God, since had He always conversed with them merely as a man, they would have continued in the same low condition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore that this may not be, He bringeth forward the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet the creation itself “worketh” on the Sabbath, (for the sun runneth, rivers flow, fountains bubble, women bear,) but that thou mayest learn that He is not of creation, He said not, “Yea, I work, for creation worketh,” but, “Yea, I work, for My Father worketh.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Therefore the Jews sought the more to kill Him, because He not only had broken the Sabbath, but said also that God was His Father, making Himself equal with God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this he asserted not by words merely, but by deeds, for not in speech alone, but also yet oftener by actions He declared it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because they might object to His words and charge Him with arrogance, but when they saw the truth of His actions proved by results, and His power proclaimed by works, after that they could say nothing against Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But they who will not receive these words in a right mind assert, that “Christ made not Himself equal to God, but that the Jews suspected this.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Come then let us go over what has been said from the beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Tell me, did the Jews persecute Him, or did they not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is clear to every one that they did.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did they persecute Him for this or for something else?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is again allowed that it was for this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did He then break the Sabbath, or did He not?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Against the fact that He did, no one can have anything to say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Did He call God His Father, or did He not call Him so?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This too is true.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then the rest also follows by the same consequence; for as to call God His Father, to break the Sabbath, and to be persecuted by the Jews for the former and more especially for the latter reason, belonged not to a false imagination, but to actual fact, so to make Himself equal to God was a declaration of the same meaning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this one may see more clearly from what He had before said, for “My Father worketh, and I work,” is the expression of One declaring Himself equal to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For in these words He has marked no difference.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “He worketh, and I minister,” but, “As He worketh, so work I”; and hath declared absolute Equality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if He had not wished to establish this, and the Jews had supposed so without reason, He would not have allowed their minds to be deceived, but would have corrected this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, the Evangelist would not have been silent on the subject, but would have plainly said that the Jews supposed so, but that Jesus did not make Himself equal to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As in another place he doth this very thing, when he perceiveth that something was said in one way, and understood in another; as, “Destroy this Temple,” said Christ, “and in three days I will raise It up” ( c. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 19 ); speaking of His Flesh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the Jews, not understanding this, and supposing that the words were spoken of the Jewish Temple, said, “Forty and six years was this temple in building, and wilt Thou rear it up in three days?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then He said one thing, and they imagined another, (for He spake of His Flesh, and they thought that the words were spoken of their Temple,) the Evangelist remarking on this, or rather correcting their imagination, goes on to say, “But He spake of the Temple of His Body.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that here also, if Christ had not made Himself equal with God, had not wished to establish this, and yet the Jews had imagined that He did, the writer would here also have corrected their supposition, and would have said, “The Jews thought that He made Himself equal to God, but indeed He spake not of equality.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this is done not in this place only, nor by this Evangelist only, but again elsewhere another Evangelist is seen to do the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when Christ warned His disciples, saying, “Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees” ( Matt. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 6 ), and they reasoned among themselves, saying, “It is because we have taken no bread,” and He spake of one thing, calling their doctrine “leaven,” but the disciples imagined another, supposing that the words were said of bread; it is not now the Evangelist who setteth them right, but Christ Himself, speaking thus, “How is it that ye do not understand, that I spake not to you concerning bread?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But here there is nothing of the kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith some one, “to remove this very thought Christ has added,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 19. “‘The Son can do nothing of Himself.’”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He doth the contrary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith this not to take away, but to confirm, His Equality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But attend carefully, for this is no common question.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The expression “of Himself” is found in many places of Scripture, with reference both to Christ and to the Holy Ghost, and we must learn the force of the expression, that we may not fall into the greatest errors; for if one take it separately by itself in the way in which it is obvious to take it, consider how great an absurdity will follow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not that He could do some things of Himself and that others He could not, but universally,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The Son can do nothing of Himself.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I ask then my opponent, “Can the Son do nothing of Himself, tell me?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If he reply, “that He can do nothing,” we will say, that He hath done of Himself the very greatest of all goods.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As Paul cries aloud, saying, “Who being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God, but made Himself of no reputation, and took upon Him the form of a servant.” ( Phil. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, Christ Himself in another place saith, “I have power to lay down My life, and I have power to take it again”: and, “No man taketh it from Me, but I lay it down of Myself.” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that He hath power over life and death, and that He wrought of Himself so mighty a Dispensation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And why speak I concerning Christ, when even we, than whom nothing can be meaner, do many things of ourselves?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Of ourselves we choose vice, of ourselves we go after virtue, and if we do it not of ourselves, and not having power, we shall neither suffer hell if we do wrong, nor enjoy the Kingdom if we do right.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then meaneth, “Can do nothing of Himself”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That He can do nothing in opposition to the Father, nothing alien from, nothing strange to Him, which is especially the assertion of One declaring an Equality and entire agreement.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore said He not, that “He doeth nothing contrary,” instead of, “He cannot do”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was that from this again He might show the invariableness and exactness of the Equality, for the expression imputes not weakness to Him, but even shows His great power; since in another place Paul saith of the Father, “That by two immutable things in which it was impos sible for God to lie” ( Heb. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 18 ): and again, “If we deny Him — He abideth faithful,” for “He cannot deny Himself.” ( 2 Tim. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And in truth this expression, “impossible,” is not declaratory of weakness, but power, power unspeakable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what He saith is of this kind, that “that Essence admitteth not such things as these.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For just as when we also say, “it is impossible for God to do wrong,” we do not impute to Him any weakness, but confess in Him an unutterable power; so when He also saith, “I can of Mine own Self do nothing” ( v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 30 ), His meaning is, that “it is impossible, nature admits not, that I should do anything contrary to the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that you may learn that this is really what is said, let us, going over what follows, see whether Christ agreeth with what is said by us, or among you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou sayest, that the expression does away with His Power and His proper Authority, and shows His might to be but weak; but I say, that this proves His Equality, His unvarying Likeness, (to the Father,) and the fact that all is done as it were by one Will and Power and Might.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then enquire of Christ Himself, and see by what He next saith whether He interpreteth these words according to thy supposition or according to ours.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For what things soever the Father doeth these also doeth the Son likewise.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how He hath taken away your assertion by the root, and confirmed what is said by us? since, if Christ doeth nothing of Himself, neither will the Father do anything of Himself, if so be that Christ doeth all things in like manner to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If this be not the case, another strange conclusion will follow.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He said not, that “whatsoever things He saw the Father do, He did,” but, “except He see the Father doing anything, He doeth it not”; extending His words to all time; now He will, according to you, be continually learning the same things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how exalted is the idea, and that the very humility of the expression compelleth even the most shameless and unwilling to avoid groveling thoughts, and such as are unsuited to His dignity?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For who so wretched and miserable as to assert, that the Son learneth day by day what He must do? and how can that be true, “Thou art the same, and Thy years shall not fail”? ( Ps. cii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 27 ), or that other, “All things were made by Him, and without Him was not anything made” ( c. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 3 ); if the Father doeth certain things, and the Son seeth and imitateth Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that from what was asserted above, and from what was said afterwards, proof is given of His independent Power? and if He bringeth forward some expressions in lowly manner, marvel not, for since they persecuted Him when they had heard His exalted sayings, and deemed Him to be an enemy of God, sinking a little in expression alone, He again leadeth His discourse up to the sublimer doctrines, then in turn to the lower, varying His teaching that it might be easy of acceptance even to the indisposed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe, after saying, “My Father worketh, and I work”; and after declaring Himself equal with God, He addeth, “The Son can do nothing of Himself, but what He seeth the Father do.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then again in a higher strain, “What things soever the Father doeth, these also doeth the Son likewise.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then in a lower,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The Father loveth the Son, and showeth Him all things that Himself doeth; and He will show Him greater works than these.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how great is the humility of this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And with reason; for what I said before, what I shall not cease to say, I will now repeat, that when He uttereth anything low or humbly, He putteth it in excess, that the very poverty of the expression may persuade even the indisposed to receive the notions with pious understanding.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since, if it be not so, see how absurd a thing is asserted, making the trial from the words themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when He saith, “And shall show Him greater works than these,” He will be found not to have yet learned many things, which cannot be said even of the Apostles; for they when they had once received the grace of the Spirit, in a moment both knew and were able to do all things which it was needful that they should know and have power to do, while Christ will be found to have not yet learned many things which He needed to know.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what can be more absurd than this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then is His meaning?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was because He had strengthened the paralytic, and was about to raise the dead, that He thus spake, all but saying, “Wonder ye that I have strengthened the paralyzed?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Ye shall see greater things than these.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He spake not thus, but proceeded somehow in a humbler strain, in order that He might soothe their madness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that thou mayest learn that “shall show” is not used absolutely, listen again to what followeth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For as the Father raiseth up the dead, and quickeneth them, even so the Son quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet “can do nothing of Himself” is opposed to “whom He will”: since if He quickeneth “whom He will,” He can do something “of Himself,” (for to “will” implies power,) but if He “can do nothing of Himself,” then He can not “quicken whom He will.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the expression, “as the Father raiseth up,” showeth unvarying resemblance in Power, and “whom He will,” Equality of Authority.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou therefore that “cannot do anything of Himself” is the expression of One not taking away His (own) authority, but declaring the unvarying resemblance of His Power and Will (to those of the Father)?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In this sense also understand the words, “shall show to Him”; for in another place He saith, “I will raise him up at the last Day.” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, to show that He doth it not by receiving an inward power from above, He saith, “I am the Resurrection and the Life.” ( c. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then that thou mayest not assert that He raiseth what dead He will and quickeneth them, but that He doth not other things in such manner, He anticipateth and preventeth every objection of the kind by saying, “What things soever He doeth, these also doeth the Son likewise,” thus declaring that He doeth all things which the Father doeth, and as the Father doeth them; whether thou speakest of the raising of the dead, or the fashioning of bodies, or the remission of sins, or any other matter whatever, He worketh in like manner to Him who begat Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But men careless of their salvation give heed to none of these things; so great an evil is it to be in love with precedence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This has been the mother of heresies, this has confirmed the impiety of the heathen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For God desired that His invisible things should be understood by the creation of this world ( Rom. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 20 ), but they having left these and refused to come by this mode of teaching, cut out for themselves another way, and so were cast out from the true.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And the Jews believed not because they received honor from one another, and sought not the honor which is from God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But let us, beloved, avoid this disease exceedingly and with all earnestness; for though we have ten thousand good qualities, this plague of vainglory is sufficient to bring them all to nought. ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If therefore we desire praise, let us seek the praise which is from God, for the praise of men of what kind soever it be, as soon as it has appeared has perished, or if it perish not, brings to us no profit, and often proceeds from a corrupt judgment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what is there to be admired in the honor which is from men? which young dancers enjoy, and abandoned women, and covetous and rapacious men?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But he who is approved of God, is approved not with these, but with those holy men the Prophets and Apostles, who have shown forth an angelic life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If we feel any desire to lead multitudes about with us or be looked at by them, let us consider the matter apart by itself, and we shall find that it is utterly worthless.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In fine, if thou art fond of crowds, draw to thyself the host of angels, and become terrible to the devils, then shalt thou care nothing for mortal things, but shalt tread all that is splendid underfoot as mire and clay; and shall clearly see that nothing so fits a soul for shame as the passion for glory; for it cannot, it cannot be, that the man who desires this should live the crucified life, as on the other hand it is not possible that the man who hath trodden this underfoot should not tread down most other passions; for he who masters this will get the better of envy and covetousness, and all the grievous maladies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And how,” saith some one, “shall we get the better of it?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If we look to the other glory which is from heaven, and from which this kind strives to cast us out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that heavenly glory both makes us honored here, and passes with us into the life which is to come, and delivers us from all fleshly slavery which we now most miserably serve, giving up ourselves entirely to earth and the things of earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if you go into the forum, if you enter into a house, into the streets, into the soldiers’ quarters, into inns, taverns, ships, islands, palaces, courts of justice, council chambers, you shall everywhere find anxiety for things present and belonging to this life, and each man laboring for these things, whether gone or coming, traveling or staying at home, voyaging, tilling lands, in the fields, in the cities, in a word, all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What hope then of salvation have we, when inhabiting God’s earth we care not for the things of God, when bidden to be aliens from earthly things we are aliens from heaven and citizens of earth?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What can be worse than this insensibility, when hearing each day of the Judgment and of the Kingdom, we imitate the men in the days of Noah, and those of Sodom, waiting to learn all by actual experience?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet for this purpose were all those things written, that if any one believe not that which is to come, he may, from what has already been, get certain proof of what shall be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Considering therefore these things, both the past and the future, let us at least take breath a little from this hard slavery, and make some account of our souls also, that we may obtain both present and future blessings; through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom, with the Father and the Holy Ghost, be glory, now and ever, and world without end.
